task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. Empty lists, one-element lists, and two-element lists return None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code handles the edge case of lists with two elements correctly.
trace: ** The function `trimmed_mean` first checks if the list has two or fewer elements, in which case it returns `None` as per the requirement. For lists with more than two elements, it removes one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum value using the `remove()` method. Finally, it calculates and returns the mean of the remaining numbers by dividing their sum by the count of the remaining numbers, ensuring the result is returned as a float.
